verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has a history of hypertension.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This is from the chart the patient's prior history. "Essential (primary) hypertension
" Tab-Diagnoses, Column-I, Row-295.

verifier_values.criteria: Recognizes the patient's blood pressure is uncontrolled despite medications.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This is from the script. "Physician: Oh yes It was high today. The nurse recorded it at 150/80
Patient: Yes I thought it was high too but when I went to the emergency department they said it didn’t need to be lowered there." 

The prompt also states that the patient has failed and is not able to tolerate multiple antihypertensives, and clearly states that he has failed first line antihypertensives.

"Table 4. Categories of Blood Pressure in Adults* SBP BP Category Normal Elevated Hypertension Stage 1 Stage 2 <120 mm Hg 120 to 129 mm Hg 130 to 139 mm Hg ≥140 mm Hg DBP and and or or <80 mm Hg <80 mm Hg 80 to 89 mm Hg ≥90 mm Hg" https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1161/CIR.0000000000001356

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's renal function is above the cutoff for antihypertensives
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
The patient's renal function appears acceptable, with a normal creatinine and potassium. This does not limit his choices of antihypertensives further. 
His calculated eGFR is 59ml/min/1.73m², which is more than 45, not limiting his choices as per the guidelines as well (which holds 45ml/min/1.73m² as a cut off for antihypertensives choice in resistant hypertension).

verifier_values.criteria: Utilizes the 2025 American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association joint committee clinical practice guidelines on hypertension.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
"2025 AHA/ACC/AANP/AAPA/ABC/ACCP/ACPM/AGS/AMA/ASPC/NMA/PCNA/SGIM Guideline for the Prevention, Detection, Evaluation and Management of High Blood Pressure in Adults: A Report of the American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association Joint Committee on Clinical Practice Guidelines

The “2025 AHA/ACC/AANP/AAPA/ABC/ACCP/ACPM/AGS/AMA/ASPC/NMA/PCNA/SGIM Guideline for the Prevention, Detection, Evaluation, and Management of High Blood Pressure in Adults” retires and replaces the “2017 ACC/AHA/AAPA/ABC/ACPM/AGS/APhA/ASH/ASPC/NMA/PCNA Guideline for the Prevention, Detection, Evaluation, and Management of High Blood Pressure in Adults.”"

https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/10.1161/CIR.0000000000001356

verifier_values.criteria: Recognizes that mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist (MRA) therapy is recommended where first-line antihypertensive therapy has failed.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: "In adults with uncontrolled resistant hypertension despite optimal treatment with first-line antihypertensive therapy (ie, a combination of ACEi or ARB plus CCB and thiazide-like diuretic [chlorthalidone or indapamide] and with an eGFR of ≥45 mL/min/1.73 m2), addition of a MRA is recommended to control BP.6" https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1161/CIR.0000000000001356

verifier_values.criteria: States the patient does not tolerate MRA.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is from the prompt. "Spironolactone, amiloride, and eplerenone are not tolerated." - where Spironolactone and eplerenone are MRAs

verifier_values.criteria: States amiloride is an option when MRA is not tolerated.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: " In adults with uncontrolled resistant hypertension who cannot tolerate or have contraindications to MRA, the addition of one of the following agents or classes— amiloride, BBs, alpha-blockers, central sympatholytic drugs, dual endothelin receptor antagonists, or direct vasodilators—is reasonable to control BP. " https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1161/CIR.0000000000001356

verifier_values.criteria: States the patient does not tolerate amiloride.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is from the prompt. "Spironolactone, amiloride, and eplerenone are not tolerated."

" https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1161/CIR.0000000000001357

This is from the chart  "metoprolol tartrate (LOPRESSOR) 25 MG tablet Take 25 mg by mouth daily"
" Tab-Clinical Notes, Note type- Progress Note, Column-D, Row-185, Section Current Outpatient Medications. "Metoprolol is in a class of medications called beta blockers."https://medlineplus.gov/druginfo/meds/a682864.html

verifier_values.criteria: States the patient can be offered either of the following:alpha blockers, central sympatholytic drugs, dual endothelin receptor antagonists, or direct vasodilators.

" https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/pdf/10.1161/CIR.0000000000001361 Since the patient is already on a beta blocker, other agents or classes should be tried.
